# gcore_cloud_ssh_keys (Data Source)

SSH key pairs provide secure authentication to cloud instances, supporting both generated and imported public keys.

## Example Usage

```terraform
data "gcore_cloud_ssh_keys" "example_cloud_ssh_keys" {
  project_id = 1
  name = "my-ssh-key"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Optional

- `max_items` (Number) Max items to fetch, default: 1000
- `name` (String) SSH key name. Partial substring match. Example: `name=abc` matches any key containing `abc` in name.
- `order_by` (String) Sort order for the SSH keys
Available values: "created_at.asc", "created_at.desc", "name.asc", "name.desc".
- `project_id` (Number) Project ID

### Read-Only

- `items` (Attributes List) The items returned by the data source (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--items))

<a id="nestedatt--items"></a>
### Nested Schema for `items`

Read-Only:

- `created_at` (String) SSH key creation time
- `fingerprint` (String) Fingerprint
- `id` (String) SSH key ID
- `name` (String) SSH key name
- `project_id` (Number) Project ID
- `public_key` (String) The public part of an SSH key is the shareable portion of an SSH key pair. It can be safely sent to servers or services to grant access. It does not contain sensitive information.
- `shared_in_project` (Boolean) SSH key will be visible to all users in the project
- `state` (String) SSH key state
Available values: "ACTIVE", "DELETING".
